tests.source_program:
define <4 x i1> @test_vector_pow2_masks(<4 x i32> %a) {
  %m1 = shl <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>, <i32 0, i32 1, i32 2, i32 3>
  %m2 = shl <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>, <i32 4, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  %t1 = and <4 x i32> %a, %m1
  %t2 = and <4 x i32> %a, %m2
  %c1 = icmp eq <4 x i32> %t1, zeroinitializer
  %c2 = icmp eq <4 x i32> %t2, zeroinitializer
  %result = or <4 x i1> %c1, %c2
  ret <4 x i1> %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define <4 x i1> @test_vector_pow2_masks(<4 x i32> %a) {
  %m1 = shl <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>, <i32 0, i32 1, i32 2, i32 3>
  %m2 = shl <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>, <i32 4, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  %mask = or <4 x i32> %m1, %m2
  %t = and <4 x i32> %a, %mask
  %result = icmp ne <4 x i32> %t, %mask
  ret <4 x i1> %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary does not mention vector type support for the power-of-two mask optimization, only scalar cases are discussed

define <4 x i1> @test_vector_pow2_masks(<4 x i32> %a) {
  %m1 = shl <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>, <i32 0, i32 1, i32 2, i32 3>
  %m2 = shl <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 1, i32 1>, <i32 4, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7>
  %t1 = and <4 x i32> %a, %m1
  %t2 = and <4 x i32> %a, %m2
  %c1 = icmp eq <4 x i32> %t1, zeroinitializer
  %c2 = icmp eq <4 x i32> %t2, zeroinitializer
  %result = or <4 x i1> %c1, %c2
  ret <4 x i1> %result
}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_select_derived_pow2_masks(i32 %a, i1 %cond) {
  %m1 = select i1 %cond, i32 2, i32 4
  %m2 = select i1 %cond, i32 8, i32 16
  %t1 = and i32 %a, %m1
  %t2 = and i32 %a, %m2
  %c1 = icmp eq i32 %t1, 0
  %c2 = icmp eq i32 %t2, 0
  %result = or i1 %c1, %c2
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @test_select_derived_pow2_masks(i32 %a, i1 %cond) {
  %m1 = select i1 %cond, i32 2, i32 4
  %m2 = select i1 %cond, i32 8, i32 16
  %mask = or i32 %m1, %m2
  %t = and i32 %a, %mask
  %result = icmp ne i32 %t, %mask
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions SimplifyQuery for non-constant masks but does not cover masks derived from select instructions with power-of-two values

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_ne_and_with_freeze_insertion(i32 %a, i32 %s1, i32 %s2) {
  %m1 = shl i32 1, %s1
  %m2 = shl i32 1, %s2
  %t1 = and i32 %a, %m1
  %t2 = and i32 %a, %m2
  %c1 = icmp ne i32 %t1, 0
  %c2 = icmp ne i32 %t2, 0
  %result = and i1 %c1, %c2
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @test_ne_and_with_freeze_insertion(i32 %a, i32 %s1, i32 %s2) {
  %m1 = shl i32 1, %s1
  %m2 = shl i32 1, %s2
  %m2_frozen = freeze i32 %m2
  %mask = or i32 %m1, %m2_frozen
  %t = and i32 %a, %mask
  %result = icmp eq i32 %t, %mask
  ret i1 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions freeze insertion for logical operations but does not provide concrete examples showing where freeze is actually inserted in the optimized output

  %c1 = icmp ne i32 %t1, 0

  %m2 = shl nuw i32 1, %s2
  %1 = or i32 %m1, %m2
  %2 = and i32 %a, %1

  ret i1 %result

define i1 @test_ne_and_with_freeze_insertion(i32 %a, i32 %s1, i32 %s2) {
  %m1 = shl nuw i32 1, %s1
  %m2 = shl nuw i32 1, %s2
  %1 = or i32 %m1, %m2
  %2 = and i32 %a, %1
  %result = icmp eq i32 %2, %1
  ret i1 %result
}
